title: Tattershall (1) Lancaster Memorial
crew_summary: 7 killed including pilot Flg Off. Peter Hilary Norton
narrative: Lancaster W4175 had survived the long and dangerous round trip to Berlin -- one of the most heavily defended targets in Germany. The crew must have felt relief as they crossed the English coast and made for their home airfield. But tragedy struck at the last moment. While circling RAF Woodhall Spa preparing to land, the aircraft crashed onto the Tattershall and Coningsby Gas Works, killing all six crew instantly. The cruel irony of surviving a Berlin raid only to die within sight of home was a fate that befell more Bomber Command crews than the popular narrative acknowledges. Many losses occurred during landing, in circuit, or on approach -- exhausted crews flying damaged aircraft in poor visibility after hours of operational stress.
cause_of_loss: Crashed while circling the airfield on return
operation_target: Returning from bombing raid on Berlin
memorial_details: A memorial plaque to the crew is located on the nearby Butt's Bridge. It was erected by the Royal British Legion Coningsby, Tattershall and District Branch. The plaque is publicly accessible on the bridge.

external_narratives.summary: Heritage record for the crash site of Lancaster W4175 at the Tattershall and Coningsby Gas Works. Documents the aircraft crashing while circling RAF Woodhall Spa after returning from a Berlin raid on 30 March 1943.
external_narratives.key_details: Monument reference MLI125799. The heritage record notes the crash occurred on the gas works site. All six crew killed.

external_narratives.summary: Accident database entry for Avro Lancaster Mk I W4175 of 97 Squadron, recording the crash on 30 March 1943 at Tattershall while returning from operations.
external_narratives.key_details: Aircraft designated OF-U. Based at RAF Woodhall Spa. Crashed on the gas works during circuit.

external_narratives.summary: Registry entry for the memorial plaque on Butt's Bridge at Coningsby, commemorating the crews of both Lancaster W4175 (OF-U) and Lancaster PB422 (OF-P) of 97 Squadron.
external_narratives.key_details: Combined memorial on Butt's Bridge commemorates crews of two separate 97 Squadron crashes at Tattershall. Erected by the Royal British Legion Coningsby, Tattershall and District Branch.
